steps.title: Read the logical setup
steps.content: Sentence 1 says that, given the ideas circulating among British scientists in the 1800s, natural selection was "an obvious next step." In other words, the discovery was almost bound to happen.

steps.title: Identify what the blank must signal
steps.content: Sentence 2 draws a consequence from that setup: if the theory was an obvious next step, then it follows that two scientists arriving at it independently was not a coincidence. The transition needs to mark a logical consequence.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — however,: signals contrast, but sentence 2 agrees with and extends sentence 1 rather than opposing it. ✗
  • Choice B — then,: signals logical consequence — the conclusion that follows from sentence 1's premise. Fits perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — moreover,: signals an additional independent point, but sentence 2 is a consequence of sentence 1, not a separate addition. ✗
  • Choice D — for example,: sentence 2 is not an example of the prior claim; it is the inference drawn from it. ✗
B is correct.
